name: math-explorer
description: Explores an optimization-constant problem: reads the canonical constants/<id>.md ledger, fetches and digests the papers behind the current record upper/lower bounds, and reports where the gap is most exploitable and which bound is the softer target. Call at the start of each round before any attempt. Does NOT attempt the improvement.

name: proof-outliner
description: Surveys multiple attack angles for improving one bound: how the current record was achieved, analogous results and techniques worth borrowing, and the skeleton of each candidate line (analytic argument, explicit construction, or computational certificate). Produces a ranked set of approaches with the hard steps identified, not a single finished plan. Call after the explorer, once per problem per round.

name: outline-reviewer
description: Reviews a proof outline BEFORE the details are filled in. Checks for the wrong technique, unjustified leaps, missing cases, and circular reasoning. Call on every outline the proof-outliner marks 'Spec review: required'.

name: proof-builder
description: Turns a chosen approach into a concrete bound improvement: a rigorous analytic argument, an explicit construction, or a computational certificate built and checked with Bash. Produces the candidate improvement and its verification artifact in the problem's research folder. Call after the outline (and its review) are ready.

name: proof-reviewer
description: Adversarially verifies a candidate bound improvement: re-runs the certificate, independently re-derives the key inequality, confirms the claimed bound actually beats the value in the canonical table, and assigns a verification level (the repo's * minimally-verified convention). Decides whether the improvement holds and returns an APPROVE / CHANGES REQUESTED / RETHINK verdict. Call after every proof-builder attempt.
